findings.issue: android:fullBackupContent="false" uses a boolean-string value. This attribute conventionally references an XML rules resource (e.g. @xml/backup_rules), not a literal "false". Because android:allowBackup="false" is already set, auto-backup is disabled regardless, so the TAC-12 security intent holds. However, on future Android versions or if allowBackup is ever flipped to true (e.g. for a partial backup feature), the misconfigured fullBackupContent value would be silently ignored and could lead to unintended backup of app data. Consider replacing with a proper no-op rules file reference, or simply drop the attribute since allowBackup=false already covers Phase 1.
findings.fix: Option A (preferred — explicit intent): add res/xml/backup_rules.xml with <full-backup-content><exclude domain="root" path="."/></full-backup-content> and set android:fullBackupContent="@xml/backup_rules" android:dataExtractionRules="@xml/data_extraction_rules". Option B (minimal): remove the android:fullBackupContent="false" line since android:allowBackup="false" already blocks auto-backup; keep TAC-12 grep satisfied by adding the attribute only when a real rules file exists. If you keep it as-is to pass TAC-12 grep verbatim, document the rationale in decisions.md so future reviewers don't treat it as an oversight.

findings.issue: SanctumApplication.onCreate() writes to DefaultDownloadRepository.mainActivityFqn — a process-global mutable static field (Decision T6). Within the app process this is not externally exploitable (the field is not exposed across IPC boundaries and SanctumApplication.onCreate runs before any WorkManager worker starts). Still, any code path that writes to this field later — e.g. a test fixture, instrumentation, or a future module — could redirect pending-intents to an arbitrary class name, which DownloadWorker then uses to build a notification tap target. The package-prefix guard (startsWith("app.sanctum.machina.") in DownloadWorker per TAC-14) is the mitigation, so this is informational only. Re-verify the guard is in place in :core-runtime and that no other writer exists.
findings.fix: No code change required for Task 7. Ensure (during Task 8/9 reviews) that: (1) DownloadWorker validates mainActivityFqn with startsWith("app.sanctum.machina.") before Class.forName / Intent routing; (2) no other module writes to this companion field. If in the future multi-process or :app variants arise, migrate to a Hilt-provided constant rather than a mutable static.

findings.issue: The POST_NOTIFICATIONS permission launcher callback is a no-op ({ _ -> }). Denial is neither logged nor surfaced to the user. This is acceptable for Phase 1 per the task spec ("приложение должно работать без падений, notifications просто не будут показываться"), but it means if a user denies the permission, downstream features (foreground-service progress notifications for downloads) will silently fail with no audit trail. No security boundary is broken, but security-relevant logging is absent.
findings.fix: Phase 1: no change required — aligned with spec. Phase 2+: add a lightweight structured log on denial (e.g. Log.w(TAG, "POST_NOTIFICATIONS denied")) and plumb a user-visible hint into ModelManager/Chat screens so users understand why download progress notifications don't appear. Never log the permission grant result to a remote sink without user consent.

summary: Task 7 (:app bootstrap) passes security review. All TAC-12 manifest hardening attributes (allowBackup=false, fullBackupContent=false, usesCleartextTraffic=false) are present on <application>. Component export rules are correct: MainActivity exported=true is required and safe for the LAUNCHER intent-filter; SystemForegroundService exported=false prevents external invocation. POST_NOTIFICATIONS runtime request is gated by SDK 33+ and uses modern ActivityResultContracts (no legacy ActivityCompat.requestPermissions). Native libraries libOpenCL.so and libvndksupport.so are both declared required="false", avoiding install failures on devices without them. Hilt entry points (@HiltAndroidApp on Application, @AndroidEntryPoint on MainActivity) are wired cleanly, no reflection or dynamic class loading from untrusted input. No hardcoded secrets, tokens, or credentials anywhere in the diff — the only static string written to shared state is a class FQN (MainActivity), which is not sensitive. Requested permissions are minimal and well-scoped. Three low-severity observations are documented above: (1) android:fullBackupContent="false" uses a boolean-string syntax rather than an XML rules reference — functional today because allowBackup=false is the real gate, but worth tightening for defense-in-depth; (2) DefaultDownloadRepository.mainActivityFqn is process-global mutable state per Decision T6 — not exploitable externally, but relies on DownloadWorker's package-prefix guard (TAC-14) as the mitigation and must not be written from any other module; (3) POST_NOTIFICATIONS denial path has no logging — acceptable per Phase 1 scope but worth structured logging in later phases. No critical, high, or medium findings. Verdict: approve.
